column5: GSA CIO 2185.1C, Accelerating Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence at GSA
column13: Internal GSA assessment, procurement, approval, monitoring, transparency, lifecycle, risk, privacy, security, IP, interoperability, and American-made-AI governance.

column5: PIC 25-03A, Artificial Intelligence Acquisition Guidance
column13: Contracting officers must include the AI Acquisitions (SEP 2025) requirement statement in all new solicitations, contracts, and orders acquiring covered AI, and add it to extended or amended existing ones. The statement restricts use of NASA data for AI training without written authorization, prohibits training public or commercial models on nonpublic NASA data absent approval, adds vendor lock-in protections and post-contract data and model rights, requires advance notice and OCIO approval for new AI features or model updates, and requires CAIRIA risk-and-impact-assessment reporting with subcontractor flowdown.
column14: training_prohibition; data_use_restriction; data_ownership; vendor_lock_in; change_notification; impact_assessment; ai_disclosure; subcontractor_flowdown
column27: Initially issued September 30, 2025 and updated February 11, 2026; in effect until the NASA OCIO AI Guide is issued. No NFS clause text changes: obligations enter contracts through PWS, SOW, or SOO requirement statements. A companion AI-use-during-performance disclosure statement is phased in pending NASA parameter definitions.

column5: PIC 26-02, Artificial Intelligence Large Language Models Acquisition Guidance
column13: Contracting officers must include the LLM Transparency Requirements (MAR 2026) statement and LLM Transparency DRD in all new NASA solicitations, contracts, and orders acquiring LLMs or other covered AI models, and add them to extended or amended existing ones. The DRD is due with the proposal and feeds the responsibility determination under FAR 9.104-1; contractors must comply with the Unbiased AI Principles and operate a mechanism to receive and remediate end-user reports. The transparency requirements are material contract terms, and noncompliance is grounds for withholding payment and termination for cause or default.
column14: truthfulness; ideological_neutrality; model_documentation; ai_disclosure; incident_reporting; termination
column27: Implements OMB M-26-04; effective as dated and in effect until the NASA OCIO AI Guide is issued. No NFS clause text changes: requirements enter contracts through requirement statements and the LLM Transparency DRD.

column5: Acquisition Letter 2026-05, Unbiased AI Principles (transmitted by Policy Flash 2026-45)
column13: Department-wide procurement policy implementing OMB M-26-04. Requiring activities complete an AI impact assessment before solicitation; solicitations must state the AI use case, performance thresholds, bias testing, model documentation, data provenance, and lifecycle-management requirements. New solicitations, contracts, and modifications acquiring LLMs must incorporate the Ensuring Unbiased AI Principles (APR 2026) clause, whose requirements are material to eligibility and payment, flow down to all tiers, and carry cure, withholding, and termination remedies.

column24: DOE Policy Flash 2026-45, transmitting Acquisition Letter 2026-05
column27: The Policy Flash transmits the operative Acquisition Letter PDF (AL 2026-05, May 8, 2026), effective immediately until superseded. Excludes management and operating contracts for now, with separate guidance forthcoming. DOE notes that policy flashes are not themselves statements of policy; the Acquisition Letter is the operative document.
